title: Remotion · Product Lifecycle Saga (45s)
summary: A 45-second four-act narrative for a SaaS product: PROBLEM (life before), DISCOVERY (the moment of pickup), ADOPTION (the workflow inside the product), GROWTH (metrics climbing + happy customers). Each act is its own Remotion component, played back-to-back via `<Series>` so timing stays clean.
prompt:
Build a 45-second Remotion lifecycle saga (1920×1080, 30fps = 1350 frames) for a fictional SaaS product the user picks (default: a project-management tool called "Cinch").

Project layout under `.remotion-cache/<id>/`:
- `package.json` — react 18, remotion 4 + @remotion/cli
- `src/index.ts` — `registerRoot(RemotionRoot)`
- `src/Root.tsx` — `<Composition id="LifecycleSaga" component={LifecycleSaga} durationInFrames={1350} fps={30} width={1920} height={1080} defaultProps={{ brand: 'Cinch', accent: '#6cf3c0' }} />`
- `src/LifecycleSaga.tsx` — wires four acts together via `<Series>`
- `src/acts/Problem.tsx`, `Discovery.tsx`, `Adoption.tsx`, `Growth.tsx`
- `src/atoms/Headline.tsx`, `KineticLine.tsx`, `MetricRow.tsx` — small reusable atoms

Visual identity: cool slate canvas #0e1116, single accent prop (default electric mint #6cf3c0), off-white text #f5f7fa, secondary muted indigo #7da4ff for UI chrome screenshots. Inter Display 120px on headlines, Inter 24px body, JetBrains Mono 18px on data and tabular-nums.

**Act 1 — Problem (frames 0–300, ~10s)**: full-bleed dark canvas. Three pull-quote cards stacked Reddit-style enter with a 280ms `spring()` stagger from x:-80 → 0; each is a real-feeling user complaint ("I have 14 tabs open just to find today's tasks", "My team's standup is a Slack archaeology project", "By Friday I can't tell what shipped this week"). Subtle grain overlay at 8%. Hold last quote for 1s.

**Act 2 — Discovery (frames 300–600, ~10s)**: cut to a hero shot of the brand logo assembling piece-by-piece (use 5–7 absolutely-positioned spans with individual `spring()` entrances, 60ms apart). After the wordmark, a single tagline kinetic-types in below in 4 staggered lines (`<KineticLine>`) — total 1.6s. Background shifts to a subtle radial of the accent at 6%.

**Act 3 — Adoption (frames 600–1050, ~15s)**: simulated product walkthrough. Render an in-CSS three-pane app screenshot (left nav with 5 list rows, center timeline with 8 cards, right details panel). Animate four micro-interactions sequenced via nested `<Sequence>`: (a) a cursor moves to a list row and clicks (cursor is a styled `<div>` driven by `interpolate()` on x/y), (b) the row highlights and a card slides into the timeline, (c) the details panel populates with three labelled fields, (d) a green check appears bottom-right with `spring()`. Use `transform: scale()` + `box-shadow` for the click feedback — restrained motion, no bounce.

**Act 4 — Growth (frames 1050–1350, ~10s)**: a 2-column layout. Left: three `<MetricRow>` rows where the value tweens from 0 to its target with `Math.round(interpolate(frame, [0, 240], [0, target]))` and tabular-nums (e.g. 4,820 active teams; 92% weekly retention; 38h saved per team / month). Right: three small avatar+quote cards stacked with `spring()` entrances 200ms apart, each carrying a one-line testimonial. End with the wordmark + a single CTA line "`cinch.app · 14-day free`" that fades in at frame 1290 and holds.

Non-negotiables:
- Use `<Series>` to assemble acts; each act lives in its own file and uses `useCurrentFrame()` rebased to its local 0.
- All entrances are `spring()` or `interpolate()` with `extrapolateRight: 'clamp'`. No exit animations — `<Series>` handles cuts.
- Tabular-nums on every digit row.
- Min font-size 60px on headlines so YouTube thumbnails / Twitter previews stay legible.
- Pass `--composition-id LifecycleSaga` to the daemon dispatcher.

Output: `lifecycle-saga.mp4`.
